what_it_does: A Vitest integration test suite for the crossings / provenance-graph module. It creates a temporary SQLite database per test, seeds synthetic exchanges and tool_calls, runs buildCrossings, and asserts the resulting links, relation-type registry, traversal APIs, canonicalization, chunking, idempotence, and closed-sum gap accounting.
observations: Uses vitest with beforeEach/afterEach and mkdtempSync/rmSync for isolated temp databases.
Sets process.env.TEST_DB_PATH so initDatabase picks the test database.
Provides two synthetic helpers: insertExchangeRow and insertToolCall.
Tests seeded relation types (produced_by/produces, precedes/succeeds, contains/is_contained_by, references/referenced_by) and enforces directed relations must have inverses.
buildCrossings returns stats: produced, referenced, temporal, containment, toolCallsInScope, gap, gapNote, uncanonicalized.
Mechanical links get grade='mechanical' and confidence=1, with inverse stamped from the registry.
Temporal edges connect adjacent exchanges within the same session only.
Chunked build with small chunkSize preserves cross-page temporal adjacency via a carried-over previous exchange.
Containment hierarchy is project>session>exchange and is de-duplicated.
NotebookEdit uses notebook_path instead of file_path.
Tests idempotence by re-running buildCrossings and expecting zero new rows.
Traversal APIs tested: whatTouchedFile, artefactsOfExchange, whatDidSessionTouch.
Closed-sum gap accounting: produced + referenced + gap == toolCallsInScope, with a gapNote.
CanonicalizePath expands ~, normalizes .., resolves relatives against exchange cwd, and flags uncanonicalizable relative paths.
Tests that ~ and absolute paths for the same file converge to one file node.
artefactsProducedBy is asserted to be the same function as artefactsOfExchange.
standouts: Closed-sum gap accounting with explicit gapNote and uncanonicalized list — every tool_call is counted, not silently dropped.
Canonical path convergence tests: ~ and absolute forms of the same file must map to a single node.
Relation-type registry floor requiring every directed kind to declare an inverse.
Chunked pagination test that temporal adjacency across page boundaries is preserved.
conventions: One fresh SQLite database per test via temp directory and env var.
Synthetic row helpers keep tests readable and focused on behavior.
URI-style identifiers: exchange://session/lineStart, session://, project://, file://.
Stats-driven assertions rather than only row existence checks.
Mechanical edges carry grade='mechanical' and confidence=1.
concerns: Tests mutate and rely on process.env.TEST_DB_PATH, which could leak state if afterEach fails.
Some tests use platform-specific ~ expansion and os.homedir(), making them slightly environment-dependent.
Relative paths without a cwd are kept verbatim and still crossed, which may fragment the graph rather than resolve them.
The alias test for artefactsProducedBy only checks function identity, not behavior.
notes: A dense behavioral spec for a SQLite-backed provenance graph, emphasizing mechanical edge generation, inverse relations, canonical file identity, and no-silent-failure accounting.
questions: How does initDatabase decide which database path to open when TEST_DB_PATH is absent?
What is the exact SQL schema for links, relation_types, exchanges, and tool_calls?
How does buildCrossings order exchanges when chunking (timestamp vs line_start vs id)?
Are tool_input JSON paths other than file_path and notebook_path ever considered?
What downstream consumer reads gapNote and uncanonicalized, and how are gaps surfaced to users?
How are duplicate inverse links prevented on re-runs (ON CONFLICT clause or pre-check)?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is 'mechanical crossings / provenance graph', and the file tests exactly that — relation registry, link generation, traversal, canonicalization, and accounting. It matches.
apparent_themes: Provenance graph construction from operational logs
Bidirectional relation semantics with registry-enforced inverses
Closed-world / closed-sum accounting with explicit gaps
Idempotence and scale via chunked processing
intention_signals: To prove that the crossings layer can mechanically derive a complete, canonical, bidirectional provenance graph from exchange and tool-call records, with every unmapped call explicitly accounted for rather than dropped.
novelty: The combination of relation-type inverse enforcement, closed-sum gap accounting with a human-readable gapNote, and canonical-path convergence tests in a single integration suite.
contribution: A behavioral contract and regression harness for the crossings module, covering relation registry rules, mechanical edge generation, canonical file-node identity, gap accounting, bidirectional traversal, and chunked/idempotent builds.
summary_for_embedding: Vitest test file that specifies and verifies a SQLite-backed provenance graph builder linking projects, sessions, exchanges, and file artefacts through a relation-type registry, mechanical edge generation, canonicalization, and traversal APIs.
intention_for_embedding: To enforce that the crossings layer mechanically produces a complete, canonical, bidirectional provenance graph from tool-call and exchange records without silently omitting unmapped calls.

symbols.does: Inserts a synthetic exchanges row for a test scenario.
symbols.description: A helper function that populates the exchanges table with a controlled row, supplying id, project, timestamp, session_id, line_start/line_end, and optional cwd so tests can construct provenance scenarios without repeating SQL.

symbols.does: Inserts a synthetic tool_calls row with optional file_path or notebook_path JSON input.
symbols.description: A helper function that creates a tool_calls record tied to an exchange, encoding either a file_path or notebook_path in the tool_input JSON; used to drive buildCrossings and test produced/referenced/gap behavior.
